calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-26-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Paga Settlement; And, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
Off Calendar May-02-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KYLE R. NORDREHAUG, Esq. of Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik De Blouw LLP (Tel: 858-551-1223) for plaintiff;
Atty. CAROLINE P. DONELAN, Esq. of Blank Rome LLP (Tel: 424-239-3400) for defendant Blue Link Wireless, LLC.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CAMREE LYNN's Motion To Approve PAGA Settlement.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to approve PAGA settlement" is granted.

Order signed. Separate judgment signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2023 Continued To Jun-21-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2022 Continued To Apr-19-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SERGIO JULIAN PUCHE, Esq. of Blumenthal Nordrehaug Bhowmik de Blouw LLP (Tel: 858-551-1223) for plaintiff;
Atty. CAROLINE DONELAN, Esq. of Blank Rome LLP (Tel: 424-239-3400) for defendant Blue Link Wireless, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BLUE LINK WIRELESS, LLC'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Defendant's "motion for an order to transfer venue" is denied.

Defendant operates wireless telephone stores in California - three of them in San Francisco. Plaintiff's Private Attorney General Act (PAGA) complaint alleges that Labor Code violations occurred in the stores. The First District Court of Appeal holds that PAGA "venue is proper in any county in which an aggrieved employee worked and Labor Code violations allegedly occurred." (Crestwood Behavioral Health, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (2021) 60 Cal.App.5th 1069, 1075.)

Our defendant does not dispute that aggrieved employees worked in San Francisco or that Labor Code violations are alleged to have occurred here. Defendant nonetheless argues for venue where its principal place of business (and thus witnesses and evidence) is located and where our plaintiff worked (San Diego County). The Crestwood defendant made the same arguments, but the First District rejected them, finding that "[w]here, as here, venue is proper in more than one county, a plaintiff may choose among the available options." (Id. at 1074-75.)

Defendant has 30 days to respond.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
